identity.title: Masked Config Update 32b
identity.supported_scope: pure voltage-domain event-driven transient behavioral Verilog-A

parameters.name: vdd
parameters.override_behavior: sets the voltage-coded output high level

parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets the decision threshold for configuration and mask inputs

properties.observable_contract: For each bit N, out_cfg[N] equals new_cfg[N] when mask[N] is high and equals old_cfg[N] when mask[N] is low.
With every mask bit low, the complete output word equals old_cfg.
With every mask bit high, the complete output word equals new_cfg.
Changing mask or data bit N affects only out_cfg[N]; bus indices are neither reversed nor shifted.
Each output bit uses 0 V for logic low and vdd for logic high with finite transition smoothing.

modeling_constraints: AMS role: masked trim/configuration word update for calibration and mode-control flows.
Use deterministic combinational voltage-domain masked selection.
Preserve one-to-one bit alignment across all 32-bit buses.
Use smooth voltage contributions for every output bit.
Do not add hidden state, undeclared artifacts, debug outputs, validation hooks, or simulator-specific side channels.
